name: sandboxed_workspace_write
description: Proves the worktree bind-mount is the writable surface inside the sandbox. The agent is asked to create a file at the workspace root; the runner asserts the file landed (files_modified_any) AND that nothing else moved (files_modified_exact). Together with `sandboxed_hello.json` (smoke), this scenario exercises the read/write round-trip across the sandbox boundary: agent inside container -> /work bind -> host worktree -> runner artifact collection. A green run with `--sandbox docker` proves the only-writable-mount contract end-to-end with a real LLM driving the writes (the `test_docker_e2e.py` suite covers the same invariant at the provider layer; this scenario covers it at the orchestrator+scoring layer).

turns.message:
Create a new file at the workspace root named sandbox_evidence.txt whose only contents is the single line: written-from-inside-sandbox
Do not modify any other file. Reply with just the filename you created.
